Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". 3. **Dual lockfiles** (bun + npm) on both root and server need a stated policy.
4. **Apache reverse-proxy assumption** in `deploy.sh` (`systemctl restart httpd`) is undocumented; no virtual-host config tracked in repo.
5. **`/srv/www/AiTutor`** hardcoded path in `deploy.sh` should be documented as a deployment convention.

I now have enough context to deliver the inventory report.

# Frontend Inventory Report — `app/`

The frontend is a React Router v7 SPA (`ssr: false`), authenticated via Better Auth session cookies talking to a separate Express backend at `VITE_API_URL` (defaults to `http://localhost:4000`). All data flows through `app/lib/api.ts` (cookie-credentialed `fetch`) and per-route `clientLoader` calls guarded by `requireClientUser(role?)`.

## Top-level

- `app/routes.ts` (16 lines) — Flat `RouteConfig` array. Trivial; self-documenting.
- `app/root.tsx` (129 lines) — Root layout, `Layout`, `HydrateFallback`, `ErrorBoundary`, font preconnects, and a pre-hydration `themeScript` injected via `dangerouslySetInnerHTML` (lines 18-33) to avoid dark-mode flash. Mounts the provider stack `AuthProvider > BugReportProvider > TourProvider` (lines 91-101). Comments: sparse but present where it matters. Complexity: moderate (theme + provider order is non-obvious). Public API self-documenting.
- `app/app.css` — Tailwind/global CSS only. No comments needed.
- `app/welcome/welcome.tsx` — Default Vite/React Router scaffold; appears unused.
- `app/__tests__/**` — Vitest specs for `useLocalUser`, `Nav`, `BugReportDialog`, `BugReportProvider`, `BugReportsTab`, `lib/api`. Comment density: sparse; obvious purpose.

## Hooks (`app/hooks/`)

- `useLocalUser.tsx` (93 lines) — `AuthProvider` + `useLocalUser`. Fetches `/api/me` once on mount when no `initialUser`, uses a `cancelled` flag to avoid setState after unmount. Exposes `{ user, isInitializing, saveAuth, logout, setUser }`. Comments: none. Complexity: moderate domain logic. Hidden constraints: `saveAuth` is set but never re-memoized (relies on stable identity from React state setters); `logout` calls `api.logout` which deliberately bypasses the redirect-on-401 path in `api.ts`. **Bucket A.**
- `useCourseTopics.tsx` (99 lines) — Hook + Provider/consumer pair for instructor topic management. Uses `requestIdRef` to ignore stale responses (lines 21, 30, 35) — easy to break if refactored. Public API obvious from types. **Bucket A** (race-condition guard warrants a note).
- `useBugReportCapture.ts` (208 lines) — Monkey-patches `console.{log,warn,error}` and `window.fetch` on mount, restoring originals on unmount (lines 80-176). Maintains rolling buffers (`MAX_CONSOLE_ENTRIES=200`, `MAX_NETWORK_ENTRIES=100`) and a 5s screenshot cache. Lazy-imports `html2canvas`. Comments: sparse despite heavy global-state mutation. **Bucket A** — must document the patch/restore contract and buffer caps.

## Lib (`app/lib/`)

- `api.ts` (353 lines) — Cookie-credentialed JSON HTTP client + typed endpoint surface. Centralizes one non-obvious behavior: any 401/403 (except on `/`) triggers a hard `window.location.href = '/'` (lines 35-41). `logout` (lines 342-349) intentionally bypasses `http()` to avoid that redirect loop. `updateActivity` normalizes three legal `options` shapes (lines 184-194). Comments: sparse. **Bucket A** — public-API doc is critical here.
- `types.ts` (229 lines) — Domain types only (`Role`, `User`, `Course`, `Module`, `Lesson`, `Activity`, `Topic`, `AdminAiModelPolicy`, bug-report types, etc.). **Bucket C** (or B for a one-line file header).
- `auth-client.ts` (35 lines) — Better Auth client + `signInWithEduAi()` which uses `disableRedirect: true` then manually `window.location.assign(url)` to surface backend errors before redirect. **Bucket A** — that pattern is non-obvious.
- `client-auth.ts` (14 lines) — `requireClientUser(role?)` route guard; throws `redirect('/')`. Self-documenting. **Bucket B.**
- `server-api.ts` (73 lines) — SSR-style helpers (`fetchJson`, `loadUserFromRequest`, `requireUserFromRequest`) that forward the inbound `cookie` header. Currently unused by routes (SPA mode), but kept around. **Bucket B** with note about cookie-forwarding.
- `utils.ts` (7 lines) — `cn()` Tailwind merge helper. **Bucket C.**
- `activityForm.ts` (135 lines) — Pure helpers translating `Activity` to MCQ/SHORT_TEXT form values, with re-indexing of `correctIndex` after blank-choice removal (lines 88-95). **Bucket A** — translation rules are non-obvious.
- `tours/tour-types.ts` (30) — Type-only. **Bucket C.**
- `tours/tour-storage.ts` (10) — `markTourCompleted`, `isLessonRoute` regex. **Bucket B.**
- `tours/tour-utils.ts` (45) — `waitForElement` MutationObserver helper, `resolveStepRoute`, `readRouteFromElement` (reads `data-tour-route` from DOM). **Bucket A** (DOM-data convention).
- `tours/tour-engine.ts` (85) — Stateful session machine: `ActiveTourSession`, `findStepIndex` skipping unresolvable routes, `storeStepSelection` capturing the user's clicked card to derive the next step's route. **Bucket A.**
- `tours/tour-definitions.ts` (160) — Static tour scripts referencing `[data-tour="..."]` selectors and `data-tour-route` attributes scattered through routes. **Bucket B** with an explicit note that any UI element marked `data-tour="..."` is load-bearing.
- `tours/tour-engine.test.ts` — Spec.

## Components — application

- `Nav.tsx` (250) — Header + role-aware links + EduAI status probe (admins skip the probe to avoid 403, lines 38-58). Owns the `BugReportDialog` mount and orchestrates a screenshot capture before opening it. **Bucket A.**
- `TourProvider.tsx` (263) — Wraps driver.js. Drives navigation between steps, destroys the popover when route changes are pending (`pendingRoute`), uses `renderTokenRef` to ignore stale `waitForElement` resolves, and `suppressDestroyedRef` to avoid re-entering `clearTourState` during programmatic `destroy`. Heavy tacit knowledge. **Bucket A.**
- `TourButton.tsx` (32) — Trivial toggle. **Bucket B.**
- `StudentAiChat.tsx` (1092) — Forward-ref component (`StudentAiChatHandle.sendGuidePrompt`/`pushGuideMessage`). Manages per-tab (teach/guide/custom) chat state, model availability + student-policy filtering (lines 83-100), API key storage in `localStorage` under `ai-provider-keys`, knowledge-level + topic gating, suggested prompts, and a setup card. Calls back into `api.send{Teach,Guide,Custom}Message` with provider key and `chatId`. **Bucket A** — the largest doc-debt in the repo.
- `StudentActivityFeedbackCard.tsx` (113) — Pure presentational. **Bucket B.**
- `ProgressBar.tsx` (80) — Pure UI; props are obvious. **Bucket B.**
- `PublishStatusButton.tsx` (63) — Button + tooltip when blocked. **Bucket B.**
- `AddCourseTopicsButton.tsx` (83) — Inline create-topic form using `useCourseTopicsContext`. **Bucket B.**
- `AddActivityPanel.tsx` (347) — Activity creation form (MCQ/SHORT_TEXT, topics, modes). Uses derived-state-during-render pattern to reset selection when topics change (lines 28-40). **Bucket A** (mode flag + topic invariants).
- `ActivityDetailsCard.tsx` (148) — Memoized expander summarizing an activity. **Bucket B.**
- `EditActivityPanel.tsx` (283) — Mirrors `AddActivityPanel` for edits; shares `activityToFormValues`/`buildUpdatePayload`. **Bucket B** (logic lives in `activityForm.ts`).
- `TopicSyncMappingDialog.tsx` (128) — Dialog to remap topics no longer present after EduAI sync. **Bucket A** (sync semantics).
- `bug-report/BugReportProvider.tsx` (47) — Context wrapper around `useBugReportCapture` plus a settable `BugReportContext` (course/module/lesson/activity ids). **Bucket B.**
- `bug-report/useBugReport.ts` (10) — Hook accessor. **Bucket C.**
- `bug-report/BugReportDialog.tsx` (171) — `react-hook-form` + Zod (`min(10).max(2000)`), captures screenshot on open, posts via `api.submitBugReport`. **Bucket B.**
- `admin/BugReportsTab.tsx` (821) — Sortable table + JSON viewers for console/network, screenshot preview, status PATCH. **Bucket A** (column sort + log filtering rules).

## Components — UI primitives

- `components/ai-elements/{conversation,message,prompt-input}.tsx` — Vendored `ai-elements` building blocks (large `prompt-input.tsx` at 1228 lines), shadcn-style. Public API self-documenting via prop types. **Bucket B** for file headers; consider noting they are upstream-derived to avoid hand-editing.
- `components/ui/*` (button, breadcrumb, button-group, command, dialog, dropdown-menu, hover-card, input, input-group, select, separator, switch, textarea, tooltip) — shadcn primitives; thin wrappers over Radix + `cva`. **Bucket C** (pure re-skin/re-export with obvious variants).

## Routes (`app/routes/`)

All route modules follow the same pattern: `clientLoader` → `requireClientUser('ROLE')` → parallel `api.*` fetches → `Route.ComponentProps`. They use derived-state-during-render pattern (`if (initialModules !== prevInitialModules) ...`) to refresh local state when loader data changes — a non-obvious React 19 pattern worth a comment.

- `home.tsx` (300) — Marketing + EduAI sign-in. Redirects authenticated users via `routeForRole`. Comments sparse. **Bucket B** (with note on `extractErrorMessage` JSON-vs-text fallback at lines 32-50).
- `unsupported-role.tsx` (81) — TA dead-end with logout. **Bucket C.**
- `student.tsx` (151) — Student dashboard / course grid. **Bucket B.**
- `student.course.tsx` (195), `student.topic.tsx` (208) — Module/lesson list pages. **Bucket B.**
- `student.list.tsx` (804) — Lesson player: activity progression, MCQ/SHORT_TEXT submission, knowledge-level modal, per-activity feedback, pushes context to `BugReportProvider`, controls `StudentAiChat` via ref. **Bucket A.**
- `instructor.tsx` (519) — Instructor dashboard with EduAI course import. **Bucket A** (parseErrorMessage + optimistic publish toggle).
- `instructor.course.tsx` (380) — Module list + cross-course module import. **Bucket A.**
- `instructor.topic.tsx` (466) — Lesson list + module/lesson import. **Bucket A.**
- `instructor.list.tsx` (951) — Lesson editor: activities CRUD, per-activity topic + mode toggles, custom-prompt editor, EduAI topic sync with debounced "Saving…" indicators and missing-topic remap dialog. Uses `useOptimistic`. **Bucket A** (the highest-complexity instructor route).
- `admin.tsx` (1063) — Admin console: users/courses/enrollments, EduAI API key, AI model policy (allowed tutor models, dual-loop, supervisor iterations), bug reports tab. Uses optional-method probing (`getAdminSettingsApi`) so older backends degrade gracefully (lines 46-69). **Bucket A.**

## Categorization summary

**A — Detailed JSDoc / inline comments needed (complex logic, non-obvious behavior, or load-bearing public API)**
- `app/lib/api.ts` (401 redirect convention, options normalization, logout bypass)
- `app/lib/auth-client.ts` (`disableRedirect` + manual `window.location.assign`)
- `app/lib/activityForm.ts` (correctIndex re-mapping)
- `app/hooks/useLocalUser.tsx` (cancelled flag, init flow)
- `app/hooks/useCourseTopics.tsx` (`requestIdRef` race guard)
- `app/hooks/useBugReportCapture.ts` (global console/fetch monkey-patch + buffers)
- `app/lib/tours/tour-engine.ts`, `app/lib/tours/tour-utils.ts`
- `app/components/TourProvider.tsx` (driver.js lifecycle, render tokens)
- `app/components/Nav.tsx` (admin probe skip, screenshot-before-open)
- `app/components/StudentAiChat.tsx` (per-tab state, model policy, key storage)
- `app/components/AddActivityPanel.tsx` (derived-state reset)
- `app/components/TopicSyncMappingDialog.tsx`
- `app/components/admin/BugReportsTab.tsx`
- `app/routes/student.list.tsx`, all four `instructor.*.tsx` routes, `app/routes/admin.tsx`

**B — File-header purpose comment only**
- `app/root.tsx`, `app/routes.ts`
- `app/lib/client-auth.ts`, `app/lib/server-api.ts`, `app/lib/tours/tour-storage.ts`, `app/lib/tours/tour-definitions.ts`
- `app/components/TourButton.tsx`, `ProgressBar.tsx`, `PublishStatusButton.tsx`, `AddCourseTopicsButton.tsx`, `ActivityDetailsCard.tsx`, `EditActivityPanel.tsx`, `StudentActivityFeedbackCard.tsx`
- `app/components/bug-report/BugReportProvider.tsx`, `BugReportDialog.tsx`
- `app/components/ai-elements/*.tsx` (note as vendored)
- `app/routes/home.tsx`, `student.tsx`, `student.course.tsx`, `student.topic.tsx`

**C — Nothing needed (trivial / type-only / pure re-export)**
- `app/lib/types.ts`, `app/lib/utils.ts`, `app/lib/tours/tour-types.ts`
- `app/components/bug-report/useBugReport.ts`
- `app/components/ui/*` (shadcn primitives)
- `app/routes/unsupported-role.tsx`
- `app/welcome/welcome.tsx` (likely deletable)
- `app/app.css`, `app/__tests__/**`

## Cross-cutting integration notes for documenters

1. **Auth wiring**: cookies → Better Auth → Express. `api.ts` enforces redirect-to-`/` on 401/403; `client-auth.requireClientUser` enforces role. `auth-client.signInWithEduAi` deliberately disables built-in redirect to surface errors.
2. **Loaders**: every authenticated route uses `clientLoader` (no SSR loaders). React Router's `Route.ComponentProps`/`Route.ClientLoaderArgs` types come from generated `+types/*` files referenced via `./+types/<routeName>`.
3. **Provider order in `root.tsx` matters**: `BugReportProvider` depends on `useBugReportCapture` running once at the app root so it can patch `console`/`fetch` globally; `TourProvider` reads `useLocation`/`useNavigate` and must be inside the router (it is, since it's rendered inside `App` which is rendered by the router).

5. **Bug report context**: routes (e.g. `student.list.tsx`, `instructor.list.tsx`) call `setBugReportContext({...})` so submitted reports include course/module/lesson/activity ids; this should be cleared on unmount.
6. **API-key storage**: `StudentAiChat` persists per-provider keys in `localStorage` (`ai-provider-keys`) and forwards them on every chat call — this is the only spot keys are managed.
7. **Optimistic state**: instructor pages use React 19 `useOptimistic` for publish toggles and CRUD; reconciliation depends on the parent `setState` after the mutation resolves.

# Frontend Inventory Report — `app/`

The frontend is a React Router v7 SPA (`ssr: false`), authenticated via Better Auth session cookies talking to a separate Express backend at `VITE_API_URL` (defaults to `http://localhost:4000`). All data flows through `app/lib/api.ts` (cookie-credentialed `fetch`) and per-route `clientLoader` calls guarded by `requireClientUser(role?)`.

## Top-level

- `app/routes.ts` (16 lines) — Flat `RouteConfig` array. Trivial; self-documenting.
- `app/root.tsx` (129 lines) — Root layout, `Layout`, `HydrateFallback`, `ErrorBoundary`, font preconnects, and a pre-hydration `themeScript` injected via `dangerouslySetInnerHTML` (lines 18-33) to avoid dark-mode flash. Mounts the provider stack `AuthProvider > BugReportProvider > TourProvider` (lines 91-101). Comments: sparse but present where it matters. Complexity: moderate (theme + provider order is non-obvious). Public API self-documenting.
- `app/app.css` — Tailwind/global CSS only. No comments needed.
- `app/welcome/welcome.tsx` — Default Vite/React Router scaffold; appears unused.
- `app/__tests__/**` — Vitest specs for `useLocalUser`, `Nav`, `BugReportDialog`, `BugReportProvider`, `BugReportsTab`, `lib/api`. Comment density: sparse; obvious purpose.

## Hooks (`app/hooks/`)

- `useLocalUser.tsx` (93 lines) — `AuthProvider` + `useLocalUser`. Fetches `/api/me` once on mount when no `initialUser`, uses a `cancelled` flag to avoid setState after unmount. Exposes `{ user, isInitializing, saveAuth, logout, setUser }`. Comments: none. Complexity: moderate domain logic. Hidden constraints: `saveAuth` is set but never re-memoized (relies on stable identity from React state setters); `logout` calls `api.logout` which deliberately bypasses the redirect-on-401 path in `api.ts`. **Bucket A.**
- `useCourseTopics.tsx` (99 lines) — Hook + Provider/consumer pair for instructor topic management. Uses `requestIdRef` to ignore stale responses (lines 21, 30, 35) — easy to break if refactored. Public API obvious from types. **Bucket A** (race-condition guard warrants a note).
- `useBugReportCapture.ts` (208 lines) — Monkey-patches `console.{log,warn,error}` and `window.fetch` on mount, restoring originals on unmount (lines 80-176). Maintains rolling buffers (`MAX_CONSOLE_ENTRIES=200`, `MAX_NETWORK_ENTRIES=100`) and a 5s screenshot cache. Lazy-imports `html2canvas`. Comments: sparse despite heavy global-state mutation. **Bucket A** — must document the patch/restore contract and buffer caps.

## Lib (`app/lib/`)

- `api.ts` (353 lines) — Cookie-credentialed JSON HTTP client + typed endpoint surface. Centralizes one non-obvious behavior: any 401/403 (except on `/`) triggers a hard `window.location.href = '/'` (lines 35-41). `logout` (lines 342-349) intentionally bypasses `http()` to avoid that redirect loop. `updateActivity` normalizes three legal `options` shapes (lines 184-194). Comments: sparse. **Bucket A** — public-API doc is critical here.
- `types.ts` (229 lines) — Domain types only (`Role`, `User`, `Course`, `Module`, `Lesson`, `Activity`, `Topic`, `AdminAiModelPolicy`, bug-report types, etc.). **Bucket C** (or B for a one-line file header).
- `auth-client.ts` (35 lines) — Better Auth client + `signInWithEduAi()` which uses `disableRedirect: true` then manually `window.location.assign(url)` to surface backend errors before redirect. **Bucket A** — that pattern is non-obvious.
- `client-auth.ts` (14 lines) — `requireClientUser(role?)` route guard; throws `redirect('/')`. Self-documenting. **Bucket B.**
- `server-api.ts` (73 lines) — SSR-style helpers (`fetchJson`, `loadUserFromRequest`, `requireUserFromRequest`) that forward the inbound `cookie` header. Currently unused by routes (SPA mode), but kept around. **Bucket B** with note about cookie-forwarding.
- `utils.ts` (7 lines) — `cn()` Tailwind merge helper. **Bucket C.**
- `activityForm.ts` (135 lines) — Pure helpers translating `Activity` to MCQ/SHORT_TEXT form values, with re-indexing of `correctIndex` after blank-choice removal (lines 88-95). **Bucket A** — translation rules are non-obvious.
- `tours/tour-types.ts` (30) — Type-only. **Bucket C.**
- `tours/tour-storage.ts` (10) — `markTourCompleted`, `isLessonRoute` regex. **Bucket B.**
- `tours/tour-utils.ts` (45) — `waitForElement` MutationObserver helper, `resolveStepRoute`, `readRouteFromElement` (reads `data-tour-route` from DOM). **Bucket A** (DOM-data convention).
- `tours/tour-engine.ts` (85) — Stateful session machine: `ActiveTourSession`, `findStepIndex` skipping unresolvable routes, `storeStepSelection` capturing the user's clicked card to derive the next step's route. **Bucket A.**
- `tours/tour-definitions.ts` (160) — Static tour scripts referencing `[data-tour="..."]` selectors and `data-tour-route` attributes scattered through routes. **Bucket B** with an explicit note that any UI element marked `data-tour="..."` is load-bearing.
- `tours/tour-engine.test.ts` — Spec.

## Components — application

- `Nav.tsx` (250) — Header + role-aware links + EduAI status probe (admins skip the probe to avoid 403, lines 38-58). Owns the `BugReportDialog` mount and orchestrates a screenshot capture before opening it. **Bucket A.**
- `TourProvider.tsx` (263) — Wraps driver.js. Drives navigation between steps, destroys the popover when route changes are pending (`pendingRoute`), uses `renderTokenRef` to ignore stale `waitForElement` resolves, and `suppressDestroyedRef` to avoid re-entering `clearTourState` during programmatic `destroy`. Heavy tacit knowledge. **Bucket A.**
- `TourButton.tsx` (32) — Trivial toggle. **Bucket B.**
- `StudentAiChat.tsx` (1092) — Forward-ref component (`StudentAiChatHandle.sendGuidePrompt`/`pushGuideMessage`). Manages per-tab (teach/guide/custom) chat state, model availability + student-policy filtering (lines 83-100), API key storage in `localStorage` under `ai-provider-keys`, knowledge-level + topic gating, suggested prompts, and a setup card. Calls back into `api.send{Teach,Guide,Custom}Message` with provider key and `chatId`. **Bucket A** — the largest doc-debt in the repo.
- `StudentActivityFeedbackCard.tsx` (113) — Pure presentational. **Bucket B.**
- `ProgressBar.tsx` (80) — Pure UI; props are obvious. **Bucket B.**
- `PublishStatusButton.tsx` (63) — Button + tooltip when blocked. **Bucket B.**
- `AddCourseTopicsButton.tsx` (83) — Inline create-topic form using `useCourseTopicsContext`. **Bucket B.**
- `AddActivityPanel.tsx` (347) — Activity creation form (MCQ/SHORT_TEXT, topics, modes). Uses derived-state-during-render pattern to reset selection when topics change (lines 28-40). **Bucket A** (mode flag + topic invariants).
- `ActivityDetailsCard.tsx` (148) — Memoized expander summarizing an activity. **Bucket B.**
- `EditActivityPanel.tsx` (283) — Mirrors `AddActivityPanel` for edits; shares `activityToFormValues`/`buildUpdatePayload`. **Bucket B** (logic lives in `activityForm.ts`).
- `TopicSyncMappingDialog.tsx` (128) — Dialog to remap topics no longer present after EduAI sync. **Bucket A** (sync semantics).
- `bug-report/BugReportProvider.tsx` (47) — Context wrapper around `useBugReportCapture` plus a settable `BugReportContext` (course/module/lesson/activity ids). **Bucket B.**
- `bug-report/useBugReport.ts` (10) — Hook accessor. **Bucket C.**
- `bug-report/BugReportDialog.tsx` (171) — `react-hook-form` + Zod (`min(10).max(2000)`), captures screenshot on open, posts via `api.submitBugReport`. **Bucket B.**
- `admin/BugReportsTab.tsx` (821) — Sortable table + JSON viewers for console/network, screenshot preview, status PATCH. **Bucket A** (column sort + log filtering rules).

## Components — UI primitives

- `components/ai-elements/{conversation,message,prompt-input}.tsx` — Vendored `ai-elements` building blocks (large `prompt-input.tsx` at 1228 lines), shadcn-style. Public API self-documenting via prop types. **Bucket B** for file headers; consider noting they are upstream-derived to avoid hand-editing.
- `components/ui/*` (button, breadcrumb, button-group, command, dialog, dropdown-menu, hover-card, input, input-group, select, separator, switch, textarea, tooltip) — shadcn primitives; thin wrappers over Radix + `cva`. **Bucket C** (pure re-skin/re-export with obvious variants).

## Routes (`app/routes/`)

All route modules follow the same pattern: `clientLoader` → `requireClientUser('ROLE')` → parallel `api.*` fetches → `Route.ComponentProps`. They use derived-state-during-render pattern (`if (initialModules !== prevInitialModules) ...`) to refresh local state when loader data changes — a non-obvious React 19 pattern worth a comment.

- `home.tsx` (300) — Marketing + EduAI sign-in. Redirects authenticated users via `routeForRole`. Comments sparse. **Bucket B** (with note on `extractErrorMessage` JSON-vs-text fallback at lines 32-50).
- `unsupported-role.tsx` (81) — TA dead-end with logout. **Bucket C.**
- `student.tsx` (151) — Student dashboard / course grid. **Bucket B.**
- `student.course.tsx` (195), `student.topic.tsx` (208) — Module/lesson list pages. **Bucket B.**
- `student.list.tsx` (804) — Lesson player: activity progression, MCQ/SHORT_TEXT submission, knowledge-level modal, per-activity feedback, pushes context to `BugReportProvider`, controls `StudentAiChat` via ref. **Bucket A.**
- `instructor.tsx` (519) — Instructor dashboard with EduAI course import. **Bucket A** (parseErrorMessage + optimistic publish toggle).
- `instructor.course.tsx` (380) — Module list + cross-course module import. **Bucket A.**
- `instructor.topic.tsx` (466) — Lesson list + module/lesson import. **Bucket A.**
- `instructor.list.tsx` (951) — Lesson editor: activities CRUD, per-activity topic + mode toggles, custom-prompt editor, EduAI topic sync with debounced "Saving…" indicators and missing-topic remap dialog. Uses `useOptimistic`. **Bucket A** (the highest-complexity instructor route).
- `admin.tsx` (1063) — Admin console: users/courses/enrollments, EduAI API key, AI model policy (allowed tutor models, dual-loop, supervisor iterations), bug reports tab. Uses optional-method probing (`getAdminSettingsApi`) so older backends degrade gracefully (lines 46-69). **Bucket A.**

## Categorization summary

**A — Detailed JSDoc / inline comments needed (complex logic, non-obvious behavior, or load-bearing public API)**
- `app/lib/api.ts` (401 redirect convention, options normalization, logout bypass)

# Backend Inventory: AiTutor `server/`

## Stack Summary
Express 5 + Prisma + PostgreSQL + Better Auth (session cookies via `better-auth/node`). Auth federates to **EduAI** via OIDC (generic OAuth plugin) and proxies AI completions through EduAI's `/chat` endpoint. There is no JWT; auth is cookie-based session lookup hydrated from the local DB.

---

## Entry & Composition

- `server/src/index.js` — 6-line bootstrap: load `.env`, `await createApp()`, `app.listen`. **Comments: none. Complexity: trivial. Self-documenting.**
- `server/src/app.js` (lines 19–95) — App factory. Critical, **non-obvious middleware ordering**: Better Auth handler is mounted on `/api/auth/{*any}` BEFORE `express.json()` (line 45) because Better Auth needs the raw body. Then `attachSession` → `requireAuth` → an **admin-isolation gate** (lines 81–95) that 403s admins out of any path not in `isAllowedAdminPath` (`/me`, `/admin/*`, `/ai-models*`). Test injection is supported via `options.mockUser`. **Comments: moderate (good JSDoc on factory). Complexity: complex composition. Needs detailed comments** explaining ordering invariants.

## Auth & Middleware

- `server/src/auth.js` — Better Auth config with custom EduAI OIDC provider. `getUserInfo` (lines 84–105) calls EduAI's userinfo endpoint and **maps the namespaced claim `https://eduai.app/role`** to local `Role` enum via `normalizeEduAiRole`. `accountLinking.trustedProviders: ['eduai']` allows auto-link. **Comments: sparse. Complexity: external integration. Needs detailed comments** on the role-claim contract, env-var fallbacks (lines 7–19), and trustedOrigins.
- `server/src/middleware/auth.js` — `attachSession` re-fetches the full DB user (line 12) so RBAC sees the up-to-date `role` (Better Auth's session may be stale). `requireAuth`, `requireRole(role)`, `requireRoles(roles[])`. **Comments: sparse. Complexity: moderate.** Needs a one-liner explaining why we re-hydrate from DB rather than trust session claims.

## Routes (`server/src/routes/`)

| File | Purpose | Comments | Complexity | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `authentication.js` | `GET /me` returns sanitized `req.user` | sparse | trivial | One handler, fine as-is |
| `courses.js` (540 lines) | Course CRUD, EduAI import, clone, publish/unpublish | sparse | **complex / external integration** | EduAI listing+import (lines 18–176), `Promise.allSettled` topic+enrollment sync after import (lines 159–168), publish cascades children false in `$transaction` (lines 500–527), role-divergent listing (PROFESSOR sees all; STUDENT sees `isPublished` + progress) |
| `modules.js` | Module CRUD + publish/unpublish | sparse | moderate | Publish requires parent course published (line 188); unpublish cascades to lessons (lines 233–243) |
| `lessons.js` | Lesson CRUD + publish/unpublish | sparse | moderate | Two-level publish guard (lines 197, 204) |
| `activities.js` (1133 lines) | Activity CRUD + AI chat (`teach`/`guide`/`custom`) + answer submission + feedback | moderate (heading comments) | **complex domain logic** | The hub: `handleAiInteraction` (lines 186–273) orchestrates supervisor settings, tutor model resolution, EduAI access token, `aiChatSession` upsert (per `userId+activityId+mode`), trace persistence, AI help metric tracking. `POST /questions/:id/answer` increments `attemptNumber` and records submission metrics (lines 829–856). At-least-one-mode validation (lines 377, 696). Legacy `prompt`→`question` mapping (line 368). Imports schemas from `../../../shared/schemas/` |
| `topics.js` | List/create topics + EduAI sync + remap | sparse | **complex** | `POST /courses/:id/topics/sync` (lines 106–152) returns `missingTopics` (local topics absent upstream); `/topics/remap` (lines 155–252) reassigns `mainTopicId` and `ActivitySecondaryTopic` rows in a `$transaction`, then deletes the old topic. Manual creation blocked for imported courses (line 81) |
| `admin.js` | Admin-only user/course/enrollment management, EduAI API key, AI-model policy, manual enrollment sync | sparse | moderate | `PATCH /admin/users/:userId/role` returns 410 GONE — roles live in EduAI (line 28). `POST /admin/courses/:courseId/sync-enrollments` rejects non-imported courses |

## Services (`server/src/services/`)

|---|---|---|---|
| `aiGuidance.js` (656 lines) | **Core AI orchestration: dual-loop tutor↔supervisor** | complex | `callEduAI` posts to `getEduAiChatUrl()` (`{EDUAI_BASE_URL}/chat`) with the user's OAuth token in `Authorization` AND their personal provider API key in body `apiKeys[provider]`. `supervisedGenerate` runs up to `maxSupervisorIterations`; supervisor JSON verdict parsed with markdown-fence stripping + 1 retry; on rejection, tutor receives `[SUPERVISOR FEEDBACK: …]` prepended (line 430). On exhaustion, returns supervisor's `safeResponseToStudent`. Uses prompt slugs `learning-prompt`, `exercise-prompt`, `supervisor-prompt` from `PromptTemplate` (these must be seeded). | `activityEvaluation.js` | Pure-function MCQ/SHORT_TEXT correctness | trivial | |
| `systemSettings.js` | Generic key/value table; `getEffectiveEduAiApiKey` (admin override → env fallback) | trivial | |
| `bugReports.js` | Validate context hierarchy (activity→lesson→module→course consistency), enforce student-enrolled / professor-instructs gate | moderate | Custom `BugReportError(status,msg)` class for clean route mapping |

## Utils, Config, Schemas

- `utils/mappers.js` — DTO shapers used by virtually every route. **Tightly coupled to frontend `app/lib/api.ts` types** (e.g. `mapActivity` normalizes `config.options` to `{choices:string[]}` and exposes `question/type/options/answer/hints/secondaryTopics/enableXMode/customPrompt/completionStatus`). **Comments: sparse. Needs a header explaining the contract.** No version markers between client/server.
- `utils/bugReportMappers.js` — Strips PII when `isAnonymous` (lines 53–63). Trivial otherwise.
- `config/database.js` — single `PrismaClient` export. Trivial.

## Should we comment every single file? — **No.**

All three subagents converged independently on a three-tier model. Commenting every file uniformly would bury the genuinely load-bearing notes under boilerplate, and the dense JSDoc on a shadcn `Button` wrapper rots faster than it helps. The codebase has clear "hot spots" of hidden behavior (auth gates, AI dual-loop, topic remap, monkey-patched globals) and large quiet zones (UI primitives, type files, CRUD handlers) — they deserve different treatment.

`★ Insight ─────────────────────────────────────`
- **Self-documenting code beats stale comments.** TypeScript types + descriptive names already communicate the *what*; comments should only carry the *why* (constraints, invariants, gotchas, integration contracts).
- **Convergent risk hot-spots:** `app/lib/api.ts` ↔ `server/src/utils/mappers.js` is a silent-breakage seam, and `server/src/services/aiGuidance.js` is the single most under-documented complex file. Both deserve disproportionate effort.
- **Operational landmines** (Entire CLI hook dependency, orphaned Dockerfile, dual lockfiles, Apache assumption in `deploy.sh`) are *not* code-comment problems — they belong in `CONTRIBUTING.md`/`DEPLOYMENT.md`.
`─────────────────────────────────────────────────`

# CONTEXT

This codebase is an AI tutoring platform:
- Frontend: React Router v7 SPA (`app/`), ssr: false, Better Auth session cookies (NOT JWT)
- Backend: Express 5 + Prisma + PostgreSQL (`server/`)
- External integration: EduAI service (OAuth provider + AI completion proxy)

A documentation pass is partially complete. Some files already have new
file-headers and JSDoc; you must NOT re-document those. Read each target file
first; if you see a `@file` JSDoc block at the top with `Responsibility:`,
`Gotchas:`, and `Related:` lines, it's already done — skip it.

Refer to these existing docs for system context (read them once at the start):
- `docs/ARCHITECTURE.md` — system overview, request lifecycle, AI dual-loop
- `docs/DEPLOYMENT.md` — production stack
- `CLAUDE.md` — project conventions
- `SYSTEM_OVERVIEW.md` — non-technical product overview

================================================================================
# STYLE RULES — STRICT
================================================================================

1. Write the WHY, not the WHAT. TypeScript types and names already say what.
2. Never reference "added for issue X" or "used by component Y" — that rots.
3. DO NOT change any code logic. Comments only.
4. DO NOT add boilerplate that restates the obvious.
5. Use this file-header template at the top of each file:

   /**
    * @file <one-line purpose>
    *
    * Responsibility: <what this module owns>
    * Callers: <who imports/invokes it>
    * Gotchas: <non-obvious behavior, hidden constraints, integration contracts>
    * Related: <cross-references — link to docs/ARCHITECTURE.md when relevant>
    */

6. Use this per-export JSDoc template ONLY for non-trivial exports:

   /**
    * <One-line summary>
    *
    * @param ... — only document non-obvious params
    * @returns ... — only if shape isn't obvious
    * @throws ... — error conditions callers must handle
    *
    * Why: <rationale for non-obvious choices>
    */

7. Inline `// ` comments only at non-obvious branches (race guards, magic
   numbers, deliberate bypasses).

================================================================================
# WORK TO DO (22 files across 3 batches)
================================================================================

For each file: Read it first, then add the file header + per-export JSDoc +
inline notes for the listed gotchas. Skip if already documented (see CONTEXT).

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
## BATCH 1: Backend services (8 files)
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

### `server/src/services/aiGuidance.js` (~656 lines — HIGHEST PRIORITY)
This is the AI dual-loop tutor↔supervisor orchestrator. Document thoroughly.
Gotchas:
- `callEduAI` POSTs to `${EDUAI_BASE_URL}/chat` with the user's OAuth token in
  the Authorization header AND their personal provider API key in body
  `apiKeys[provider]`. Per-user keys are FORWARDED, never stored.
- `supervisedGenerate` runs up to `maxSupervisorIterations` (1-5, configurable
  per AI model policy).
- Supervisor returns JSON verdict — parsed with markdown-fence stripping
  (```json ... ```) plus 1 retry on parse failure.
- On supervisor rejection, the next tutor iteration receives
  `[SUPERVISOR FEEDBACK: …]` prepended (~line 430).
- On exhaustion (max iterations without approval), returns the supervisor's
  `safeResponseToStudent` as the final answer.
- Uses prompt slugs `learning-prompt`, `exercise-prompt`, `supervisor-prompt`
  from `PromptTemplate`. These MUST exist in DB or the service throws.
- `_testExports` is exported for tests — note this in the header.
- Three modes (teach/guide/custom) map to different prompts and behaviors.

### `server/src/services/aiModelPolicy.js`
Gotchas:
- Policy stored as JSON blob in `SystemSetting('AI_MODEL_POLICY')`.
- `resolveTutorModelSelection` returns 403 if requested model is not in
  `allowedTutorModelIds`.
- Cost-tier inference is substring-based: `flash/mini/haiku` → LOW;
  `pro/opus/o1/reasoning` → HIGH. Document so future model additions can be
  classified correctly.
- Supervisor iteration cap is clamped to 1-5.
- Document the fallback chain when policy is unset.

### `server/src/services/eduaiClient.js`
Gotchas:
- Thin EduAI HTTP wrapper for `/courses`, `/courses/:id/topics`,
  `/courses/:id/enrollments`, `/ai-models`, `/chat`.
- Base URL from `EDUAI_BASE_URL` (default `http://localhost:5174/api`).
- Responses validated with Zod from `../schemas/eduai.js`.
- `getEduAiChatUrl()` is consumed by `aiGuidance.js`.

### `server/src/services/eduaiAuth.js`
Gotchas:
- Wraps `auth.api.getAccessToken({providerId: 'eduai', userId})`.
- Maps Better Auth error strings (`UNAUTHORIZED`, `ACCOUNT_NOT_FOUND`,
  `REFRESH_TOKEN_NOT_FOUND`) to a 401 "EduAI account is not linked" so callers
  return a clean response.

### `server/src/services/enrollmentSync.js`
Gotchas:
- Pulls enrollments from EduAI; creates User+Account stubs by EduAI student ID
  for users who haven't logged in yet.
- Batch-fetches users by email and accounts by `(providerId, accountId)` to
  avoid N+1.
- Pre-creates `Account` row with `providerId='eduai'` so Better Auth auto-links
  on the student's first OAuth login. This is why `accountLinking
  .trustedProviders: ['eduai']` is set in `auth.js`.

### `server/src/services/courseCloning.js`
Gotchas:
- Deep clone modules → lessons → activities + topic remap-by-name.
- Maintains `topicIdMap` and `targetTopicsByName` inside transaction so cloned
  activities point at TARGET-course topic IDs.
- If a target-course topic with the same name doesn't exist, it's created.

### `server/src/services/activityAnalytics.js`
Gotchas:
- `calculateDifficulty` is a hand-tuned heuristic:
  `helpPerStudent*15 + incorrectRate*45 + ratingPenalty*25`.
  Thresholds 35/65 → LOW/MEDIUM/HIGH. These are policy decisions, not math —
  document the coefficients so future tuning has rationale.
- Recomputes `ActivityAnalytics` aggregate row from per-student
  `ActivityStudentMetric` data.

### `server/src/services/bugReports.js`
Gotchas:
- Validates context hierarchy: activity → lesson → module → course consistency
  must hold (no mismatched IDs).
- RBAC: student must be enrolled; professor must instruct the course.
- Custom `BugReportError(status, msg)` class for clean route mapping.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
## BATCH 2: Frontend components (8 files)
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

### `app/lib/tours/tour-engine.ts` (~85 lines)
Gotchas:
- Stateful session machine: `ActiveTourSession`.
- `findStepIndex` skips unresolvable routes (e.g., when a tour step references
  a route that doesn't exist for the current user role).
- `storeStepSelection` captures the user's clicked card to derive the next
  step's route (e.g., user clicks a course card → engine remembers which course
  → next step navigates to that course's page).

### `app/lib/tours/tour-utils.ts` (~45 lines)
Gotchas:
- `waitForElement` uses MutationObserver to wait for `data-tour="..."` anchors
  to appear (handles async route renders).
- `readRouteFromElement` reads `data-tour-route` from a DOM element. Document
  the DOM-data convention: any element with `data-tour-route` feeds the
  engine's selectedRoute state.

### `app/components/TourProvider.tsx` (~263 lines)
Gotchas:
- Wraps driver.js. Drives navigation between tour steps.
- Destroys popover when route changes are pending (`pendingRoute` state).
- `renderTokenRef` ignores stale `waitForElement` resolves after step changes.
- `suppressDestroyedRef` avoids re-entering `clearTourState` during programmatic
  `destroy` calls (prevents infinite loops).
- Document each ref's purpose.

### `app/components/Nav.tsx` (~250 lines)
Gotchas:
- Header + role-aware navigation + EduAI status probe.
- Admins SKIP the EduAI probe (~lines 38-58) to avoid 403 from the
  admin-isolation gate (admins are blocked from `/api/ai-models*`).
- Owns the `BugReportDialog` mount and orchestrates a screenshot capture
  BEFORE opening the dialog (the modal would otherwise be in the screenshot).

### `app/components/StudentAiChat.tsx` (~1092 lines — HIGHEST PRIORITY in batch)
Gotchas:
- Forward-ref component exposing `StudentAiChatHandle.sendGuidePrompt` and
  `pushGuideMessage` to parent routes (mainly `student.list.tsx`).
- Per-tab chat state: teach / guide / custom (each independent).
- Filters available models by AI model policy + student-allowed list (~83-100).
- API key storage: persists per-provider keys in `localStorage` under
  `ai-provider-keys` (stringified JSON). Forwarded to
  `api.send{Teach,Guide,Custom}Message` on every call. ONLY place keys are
  managed in the frontend.
- Knowledge-level + topic gating before chat is allowed.
- Setup card shown when API key is missing for the selected model.
- Uses `chatId` returned from server to maintain `AiChatSession` continuity.

### `app/components/AddActivityPanel.tsx` (~347 lines)
Gotchas:
- Activity creation form (MCQ/SHORT_TEXT with topic + mode selection).
- Uses derived-state-during-render pattern (~lines 28-40) to reset selection
  when `topics` prop changes — generally a React anti-pattern but the correct
  fix here. Document why.
- Mode flags require at-least-one to be true (server validates too).

### `app/components/TopicSyncMappingDialog.tsx` (~128 lines)
Gotchas:
- Dialog shown after EduAI topic sync to remap activities whose topics no
  longer exist upstream.
- Backend returns `missingTopics`; user maps each to a new local topic; dialog
  POSTs to `/topics/remap`.
- User-facing half of the topics sync/remap flow (server logic in
  `server/src/routes/topics.js`).

### `app/components/admin/BugReportsTab.tsx` (~821 lines)
Gotchas:
- Sortable table of bug reports with JSON viewers for console/network logs and
  screenshot preview.
- Status PATCH (open / in-progress / resolved).
- Column sort handles null/undefined and case-insensitive string comparison.
- Log filtering: console by level; network by status code range.
- Anonymous reports show masked user data (server-side masking in
  `server/src/utils/bugReportMappers.js`).

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
## BATCH 3: Frontend routes (6 files)
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Common pattern for ALL these files:
- `clientLoader` → `requireClientUser('ROLE')` → parallel `api.*` fetches →
  returns props.
- Route component receives `Route.ComponentProps`.
- Uses derived-state-during-render pattern
  (`if (initialFoo !== prevInitialFoo) { setFoo(initialFoo);
  setPrevInitialFoo(initialFoo); }`) to refresh local state when loader data
  changes. This is a React 19 pattern — explain it once per file where it
  appears.

Use this header template for routes:

  /**
   * @file <one-line purpose>
   *
   * Route: <e.g. /student/lesson/:lessonId>
   * Auth: <required role>
   * Loads: <what clientLoader fetches>
   * Owns: <key responsibilities>
   * Gotchas: <non-obvious behavior>
   * Related: <cross-references>
   */

### `app/routes/student.list.tsx` (~804 lines)
Route: `/student/lesson/:lessonId`
Gotchas:
- Lesson player: activity progression, MCQ/SHORT_TEXT submission,
  knowledge-level modal, per-activity feedback.
- Calls `setBugReportContext({courseId, moduleId, lessonId, activityId})` so
  submitted reports include hierarchy. MUST be cleared on unmount to avoid
  stale context for next page.
- Controls `StudentAiChat` via forward-ref handle (`sendGuidePrompt`,
  `pushGuideMessage`).
- Knowledge-level modal blocks chat until student answers.

### `app/routes/instructor.tsx` (~519 lines)
Route: `/instructor`
Gotchas:
- Instructor dashboard with EduAI course import.
- `parseErrorMessage` helper handles JSON-vs-text fallback for error responses.
- Optimistic publish toggle using React 19 `useOptimistic` — reverts on server
  error.

### `app/routes/instructor.course.tsx` (~380 lines)
Route: `/instructor/courses/:courseId`
Gotchas:
- Module list with cross-course module import flow.
- Publish/unpublish: requires parent (course) published before module can
  publish; unpublish cascades to lessons (server enforces; UI should reflect).

### `app/routes/instructor.topic.tsx` (~466 lines)
Route: `/instructor/module/:moduleId`
Gotchas:
- Lesson list within a module + module/lesson import flow.
- Same publish cascade rules as `instructor.course.tsx` but one level deeper.
- MISLEADING FILE NAME: route path is `/instructor/module/:moduleId` even
  though the file is `instructor.topic.tsx` (legacy naming).

### `app/routes/instructor.list.tsx` (~951 lines — HIGHEST PRIORITY in batch)
Route: `/instructor/lesson/:lessonId`
Gotchas:
- Lesson editor: full activity CRUD, per-activity topic + mode toggles,
  custom-prompt editor.
- EduAI topic sync flow: button triggers `/topics/sync` → if
  `missingTopics.length > 0`, opens `TopicSyncMappingDialog` to remap.
- Debounced "Saving…" indicator: changes to activity fields autosave with
  ~500ms debounce.
- Uses React 19 `useOptimistic` for publish + delete operations.
- Validation: every activity needs at least one mode enabled.

### `app/routes/admin.tsx` (~1063 lines)
Route: `/admin`
Gotchas:
- Admin console: users / courses / enrollments / EduAI API key / AI model
  policy / bug reports tabs.
- AI model policy form: allowed tutor models (multi-select), default tutor
  model, supervisor model, supervisor iterations (1-5), dual-loop toggle.
- Optional-method probing (~lines 46-69): wraps newer API calls (e.g.
  `getAdminSettingsApi`) in try/catch so older backends without those endpoints
  degrade gracefully (returns null instead of throwing). Document this pattern.
- Role PATCH UI is informational only (server returns 410 GONE — roles owned
  by EduAI).

================================================================================
# EXECUTION ORDER
================================================================================

1. Read `docs/ARCHITECTURE.md` and `docs/DEPLOYMENT.md` once for context.
2. Work through batches sequentially. Within a batch, do files in the order
   listed (highest-priority first so partial completion still ships value).
3. For each file: Read → Edit (header + JSDoc + inline notes) → next file.
4. If a file already has the `@file` JSDoc with `Responsibility:`/`Gotchas:`/

5. NEVER use `--no-verify`, never bypass git hooks, never commit unless

5. Run `git diff --stat` and report total files changed + lines added.

# CONSTRAINTS
================================================================================

- DO NOT modify any code logic — comments only.
- DO NOT touch files outside the 22 listed above.
- DO NOT create new files unless the user explicitly asks.
- DO NOT add comments to UI primitives (`app/components/ui/*`,
  `app/components/ai-elements/*`), type-only files (`app/lib/types.ts`),
